carriers.description: An abstract key domain governed by key-equivalence.
An abstract value domain governed by value-equivalence.
An abstract finite mapping from key-equivalence classes to values with stable first-insertion class order.

equivalences.definition: A declared reflexive, symmetric, and transitive relation identifying live key classes; concrete representatives are unobserved.
A declared reflexive, symmetric, and transitive relation used to compare lookup and entry values.
Pointwise equality of entries class/value sequences under key-equivalence and value-equivalence.

laws.statement: lookup(put(m, k, v), k) is value-equivalent to v
if k1 is not key-equivalent to k2, lookup(put(m, k1, v), k2) == lookup(m, k2) under value-equivalence
put with a key equivalent to a live class preserves that class position and replaces its associated value; concrete key representative retention or replacement is unobserved
put with a key inequivalent to every live key class appends exactly one class/value entry

resources.rule: Retained map handles remain usable and observationally unchanged after put on them or derived handles.
